channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Glen Taylor says the T-wolves are no longer for sale. Lore and A-Rod say it's seller remorse
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The ownership transfer of the Minnesota Timberwolves slammed to a halt when Glen Taylor declared on Thursday he won't take the final step of his drawn-out $1.5 billion deal to hand Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez the majority stake because they didn't meet all of the deadlines in the
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transfer portal, NIL challenge teams to fill and maintain rosters, even as March Madness continues
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Morehead State coach Preston Spradlin wasn’t surprised when several players from his 2021 March Madness team eventually took their talents elsewhere, with a couple landing at Power Five programs. The Eagles’ return to this year’s NCAA Tournament shows he didn’t panic.
item.dc:creator: GARY B. GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rays' Wander Franco placed on administrative leave through June 1 as sexual abuse probe continues
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Tampa Bay All-Star shortstop Wander Franco was placed on administrative leave through June 1 under an agreement between Major League Baseball and the players' association while the investigation continues into an alleged relationship with a minor.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Do you have any Astros Superstitions? Revisiting rituals on Opening Day
item.description: Astros fans are in need of luck as the team could use all the help and support they can get to win big in Arlington this week.
item.dc:creator: Michael Horton, Ana Gonzalez, KPRC 2

item.title: What’s the worst seat at Petco Park?
item.description: When choosing where to watch the Padres at Petco Park, you may want to avoid a particular seat, according to a recent study.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: MTS, Padres unveil new trolley wrap to promote public transit on gamedays
item.description: Ahead of the Padres home opener at Petco Park Thursday, the team has unveiled a new trolley wrap featuring key players in an effort to encourage fans to take the trolley.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, Fox 5

item.title: Caitlin Clark invited to play with US national team during training camp at Final Four in Cleveland
item.description: Caitlin Clark has been invited to the USA Basketball national team training camp during the Final Four in Cleveland, contingent on Iowa’s season being over.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Michael Conforto extends SF Giants’ streak of Opening Day left fielders to 18
item.description: With Conforto a free agent after this season, the streak could reach 19 next season, which would tie a 70-year-old MLB record.
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: Rays' Wander Franco placed on administrative leave amid probe into alleged relationship with minor
item.description: Major League Baseball, in a joint agreement with the players' association, has placed Tampa Bay Rays shortstop Wander Franco on administrative leave through June 1.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SF Giants’ Opening Day roster include Joey Bart, but not Marco Luciano
item.description: The Giants will carry 3 catchers to begin the season, while Nick Ahmed prevailed over Luciano in the shortstop competition.
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: Tropicana Field sold out for Tampa Bay Rays Opening Day
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rays will take on a new turf and the Toronto Blue Jays to start the season on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Sparks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Orioles to honor Key Bridge collapse victims on opening day
item.description: The Orioles will honor victims, families and first responders of the Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Nuggets star Michael Porter Jr defends brother amid gambling probe
item.description: Denver Nuggets star Michael Porter Jr. defended his brother Jontay Porter as the Toronto Raptors faces an investigation into gambling allegations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who knows what to expect, but you should feel good about these Detroit Tigers
item.description: I don't know what exactly to expect from these Detroit Tigers, but I think you should feel good about them as the season begins.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: Free Culvers cheese curds for Rays fans if they score in third inning during home games
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (WFLA) — The Tampa Bay Rays are offering a "grate" deal that will have fans "cheese-ing" hard this season. All season long, if the Rays score in the third inning at any of their home games, fans will receive free cheese curds.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pirates' Paul Skenes to donate $100 to Gary Sinise Foundation for every strikeout this season
item.description: Pittsburgh Pirates pitching prospect Paul Skenes announced Wednesday he will donate $100 for every strikeout he throws to the Gary Sinise Foundation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Gatos athletic director Ken Perrotti recognized by his peers
item.description: Los Gatos High School graduate Ken Perrotti was named by the Central Coast Section Athletic Directors Association as the 2024 Athletic Director of the Year. Perrotti will be recognized at the California State Athletic Directors Association conference on April 10-14 in San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Phil Jensen, correspondent, The Mercury News

item.title: Australian soccer club with several transgender players dominates women's tournament, sparking outrage
item.description: An Australian soccer club with apparently five transgender players won a women's preseason tournament, sparking outrage in the country and online.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Play ball! White Sox ready for home opener on the South Side as 2024 MLB season gets underway
item.description: CHICAGO — Even if summer weather isn't here yet, the Boys of Summer are back. Thursday is Opening Day on the South Side, as the Chicago White Sox embark on their 2024 campaign against the Detroit Tigers at Guaranteed Rate Field on the first full day of the Major League Baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: The 'Sho' goes on; mural of Dodger slugger Shohei Ohtani unveiled ahead of home opener
item.description: As anticipation builds for the “Boys in Blue” this season, the excitement is also, well, on the building as a giant mural of Shohei Ohtani was unveiled today in Los Angeles’ Little Tokyo just a day ahead of the Dodgers home opener. Towering 11 stories over L.A.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: New Stanford basketball coach cries while talking about resources school can provide his special needs son
item.description: (KRON) -- New Stanford men's basketball coach Kyle Smith sees the school as his dream job -- not just for what could happen on the court, but also for how the university can help his family.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Utes advance to NIT Final Four after beating VCU
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Deivon Smith recorded his fifth triple-double of the season, as the Utes basketball team advances to the NIT Final Four with a dominating 74-54 win over VCU at the Huntsman Center Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Officials admit to missed call on final play of Clippers win over 76ers
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Officials said they missed a foul call in the closing seconds of the Los Angeles Clippers' 108-107 win over Philadelphia on Wednesday night, denying the 76ers a possible victory in their fight for better playoff positioning.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN COONEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Projecting the Chicago White Sox's Opening Day lineup
item.description: CHICAGO — It's that time of year again White Sox fans. We're less than 24 hours away from first pitch on Opening Day and the South Siders just announced their 26-man roster that will take the field against the Detroit Tigers for game No. 1.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Athlete of the Week: White Plains High School senior Logan McCormick
item.description: WHITE PLAINS, N.Y. (PIX11) -- The offseason. A chance to use what you learned on the court to be better for the next year. Few players take better advantage of the time than White Plains High School's Logan McCormick.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Draymond Green ejected after arguing with referee in Warriors-Magic game
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Draymond Green hit the showers early once again on Wednesday after he was ejected from the Golden State Warriors' game against the Orlando Magic. The game was less than four minutes in when Green was tossed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Bees ready to open final season at Smith's Ballpark
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Salt Lake Bees are about to open its 30th season in downtown Salt Lake City, and its final year at Smith's Ballpark. The Bees kick off the 2024 season at Sacramento Friday with its home opener set for Tuesday against Tacoma.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: New York Mets 2024 home opener postponed due to expected rain
item.description: FLUSHING, N.Y. (PIX11) -- The New York Mets 2024 home opener has been postponed due to rain expected in the forecast, the team announced on Wednesday. The game originally scheduled for Thursday against the Milwaukee Brewers will now happen on Friday at 1:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Rizk, PIX 11

item.title: The hottest tickets in baseball this season are in Los Angeles
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers made some major investments over the winter, committing more than $1 billion to a pair of Japanese superstars, but it appears it won't take long for the club to recover the costs.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Utes 'deeply troubled and shaken' by racist incident
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The University of Utah athletics department released a statement Tuesday saying the team is "deeply troubled and shaken" by a racist incident that occurred during their stay in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho for the NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Group files lawsuit to stop Dodger Stadium gondola project
item.description: The proposal to build a skyway to link Dodger Stadium with Los Angeles Union Station and Chinatown faces another hurdle after a group of activists filed a lawsuit to have the project's environmental impact report tossed out.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Breaking rises to Olympic stage with its culture in mind: ‘We can create whatever we want’
item.description: Breaking is making its Olympic debut in Paris. The community hopes its rich culture and Bronx roots are well represented.
item.dc:creator: Sam Brief, NBC Sports, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Kansas City Chiefs cheerleader dies after childbirth complications
item.description: A former Kansas City Chiefs cheerleader has died after giving birth to her daughter, who also passed away, family members told Nexstar's WDAF.
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani says he has 'never' gambled on sports
item.description: The MLB star spoke publicly for the first time since allegations of theft and gambling opened an investigation into his interpreter.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: softball honors Amanda Scott and Donna Pickel
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) – Fresno State Athletics honored two Bulldog softball icons on Friday evening at Margie Wright Diamond. Inaugural head coach Donna Pickel, and 4x All-American and National Champion pitcher Amanda Scott.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: shortstop Ben Newton
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) –Fresno state senior shortstop Ben Newton has played a consistent role in the Bulldogs' lineup. Batting sixth he provides a spark when he's up to bat. Newton credits the Bulldogs' success this season to their offseason work and veteran group.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: How March Madness hits close to home
item.description: The Colbran Calfe was rocking as De Beque native Brennan Rigsby and 11-seed Oregon pulled away from 6-seed South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
